definitions: A paradox proposed by Zeno of Elea, suggesting that a faster runner (Achilles) can never overtake a slower one (the tortoise) if the tortoise has a head start, because Achilles must first reach the point where the tortoise started, by which time the tortoise will have moved to a new point, and so on ad infinitum.
A situation illustrating the philosophical problem of infinite divisibility of space and time.
examples: The professor used Achilles and the tortoise to explain Zeno's paradoxes.
The concept of Achilles and the tortoise challenges our intuitive understanding of motion.
We discussed Achilles and the tortoise in our philosophy class today.
synonyms: Zeno's paradox of Achilles and the tortoise
